design_philosophy.core_loop: Multi-screen action variety: horizontally scrolling combat, stationary melee fights, 3D vehicle action, maze exploration with gunplay, and precision sniper sequences
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing diverse combat scenarios and environmental hazards across fundamentally different gameplay modes while completing a linear espionage mission
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Golgo 13, a legendary assassin and problem-solver who adapts his skillset to overcome varied tactical challenges—from street-level combat to precision long-range elimination
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical rejection of single-mode gameplay in favor of screen-type modularity. Each stage type demands completely different mechanics (shooting, kicking, driving, maze navigation, sniper aiming). This signals confidence that genre variety prevents monotony and that the Golgo 13 license demands tactical versatility rather than mastery of one system

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with Game Over; implicit continues system through continues mechanic
difficulty.continues_system: Continues available; allows progression checkpoint recovery
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; completing one screen type advances to the next, preventing loss of all progress but requiring repeated attempts within stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low agency. Difficulty is inherent to stage design and enemy patterns. No documented difficulty select or adaptive scaling. Players must overcome fixed enemy configurations and timed pressure in each stage type

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon and item pickup system: ammunition replenishment during scrolling stages; infrared scope acquisition in maze sections; hand grenades in Ferrari sequence; equipment provided by CIA contacts (James and Sylvia)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven linear progression. Completion of one stage type unlocks the next. Mission objectives (eliminate Mafat leader, destroy satellite system, rescue Dr. Barrows) structure stage order. No branching paths documented
replayability_hooks: Varied stage types encourage re-engagement with different mechanics
Sniper stages require precision and wind calculation, inviting optimization
Enemy patterns and timed pressure in scrolling stages support speedrun and high-score potential
Multiple enemy types in stationary and maze sequences create tactical variation

world_building_techniques: Cold War espionage framing (CIA vs FSB blame, nuclear satellite threat)
Character-driven exposition: CIA agent James provides enemy intel and weapon support; agent Sylvia offers assistance
Mission briefing structure: rescue Dr. Barrows from Paris, eliminate Mafat leadership, destroy satellite capture system
License-based worldbuilding: Golgo 13 as a known manga/anime property brings established character authority
Geopolitical setting: London lab, Paris captivity, Mafat base, Ferrari driving across European locales

boss_design.philosophy: Named antagonist encounters embedded in stage progression. Bosses serve as mission-critical targets rather than pure combat tests. Examples include Arm of Mafat (hideout guardian), Bodyguard (protecting Ahmad Khan), and Canine (base defender). The final sniper shot on the Mafat leader is the narrative climax, requiring wind-speed precision rather than pattern memorization
notable_bosses: Arm of Mafat (hideout guardian, stationary fight)
Mafat Leader (final target, sniper precision sequence)

faction_indicators: CIA (US intelligence, provides support via James and Sylvia)
FSB (Russian intelligence, blamed for satellite incident, adversarial positioning)
Mafat (terrorist organization demanding extortion of nuclear submarines and EM research)
danger_signals: Weapons satellite falling from orbit—existential threat narrative
Nuclear warhead-equipped submarine (USS Los Angeles) as extortion leverage
Electromagnetic weapons research as geopolitical MacGuffin
Kidnapped scientist Dr. Barrows held for coercive research leverage
environmental_storytelling: London laboratory origin point for Dr. Barrows (Western research nexus)
Paris hideout as terrorist base (European espionage theater)
Ferrari driving sequence (asset deployment, mobile combat)
Maze sections with infrared scope (underground bunker/secure facilities)
Sniper nest positioning (precision elimination from concealment)
